articles.title: BASE EXPLORES ISSUING NATIVE TOKEN
articles.summary: Base creator Jesse Pollak announced at the BaseCamp event that the Coinbase-backed L2 is exploring issuing a native token, marking a reversal from the company's 2023 stance that it had no plans for tokenization. Pollak emphasized that plans remain in early stages with no timeline provided, and stressed that as a U.S. company, Base will work closely with regulators on compliance for any token issuance and distribution. The potential token announcement comes as Base has emerged as the second-largest layer-2 by total value locked at $5 billion, with impressive usage metrics including 328 million transactions over the past 30 days compared to Arbitrum's 77 million. The exploration of tokenization could trigger increased activity on Base as users attempt to position themselves for a potential airdrop, similar to patterns seen with other major L2s.

articles.title: ETHEREUM STABLECOIN SUPPLY HITS RECORD $166B
articles.summary: Ethereum's stablecoin supply has reached an all-time high of $166 billion, up from $149 billion just a month ago, further cementing its role as DeFi's settlement backbone. USDT leads with $87.8B on Ethereum, followed by USDC at $48B, signaling increasing institutional adoption. Analysts say the record supply highlights Ethereum's growing liquidity depth, resilience to macro volatility, and potential tailwinds for ETH price appreciation.

articles.title: GUILLOTINE: THE ULTRAFAST EVM IMPLEMENTATION
articles.summary: Guillotine is an Ethereum Virtual Machine (EVM) implementation written in Zig that claims to compete for the fastest EVM ever built while achieving significant size optimizations down to 110kb wasm size, representing a 4x improvement over existing options. The interpreter features data-oriented design with microoptimizations including bytecode preanalysis, instruction fusion, and tailcall recursion to maximize CPU branch prediction and minimize memory footprint, enabling universal compatibility across programming languages from Python to Swift to Rust. Guillotine represents Tevm's mission to enable Ethereum to run everywhere without compromises, utilizing techniques studied from various EVMs, including Ethereum.js, Evmone, Geth, and Revm, for differential testing. The project is currently in early alpha and released under the MIT license.

articles.title: AMERICAN EXPRESS INTRODUCES BLOCKCHAIN-BASED ‘TRAVEL STAMPS'
articles.summary: American Express' "travel stamps," part of its revamped travel app, allow customers to create commemorative records of travel experiences through ERC-721 tokens minted and stored on Coinbase's Base network. The digital passport stamps, which can be collected whenever travelers use their Amex card, are technically NFTs, but are deliberately not branded as such, as the company wanted to speak to the travel experience naturally rather than emphasize the blockchain technology. The stamps are not tradable tokens and don't function as blockchain-based loyalty points currently. They could create interesting partnership angles over time as identifiers and representations of travel history.

articles.title: DOES CRYPTO HAVE A CX PROBLEM?
articles.summary: Most crypto businesses focus on tech while customers struggle with confusing interfaces, trust issues, and poor support. Ubiquity identifies 4 key challenges killing crypto adoption [9]: complexity, UI, unsustainable growth, and negative preconceptions. Read the blog

articles.title: A GUIDE TO THE COINBASE LISTING PROCESS
articles.summary: Coinbase operates a free, merit-based digital asset listing process that begins with project teams submitting comprehensive applications, including whitepapers, team backgrounds, tokenomics details, source code links, block explorers, and third-party audit reports. The evaluation involves a business assessment examining market demand, community traction, and technical integration requirements, followed by a core review where legal, compliance, and technical security teams evaluate regulatory alignment and platform security compatibility. Upon approval, assets undergo a phased market launch starting with deposit-only periods to build liquidity, progressing through auction phases for price discovery, and culminating in full trading availability. Evaluation timelines vary significantly based on asset complexity and application completeness, with thorough submissions potentially expediting the review process. The structured approach emphasizes regulatory compliance and security standards while providing projects access to Coinbase's global user base and institutional-grade platform infrastructure.

articles.title: AAVE DAO'S STATE OF THE UNION
articles.summary: Aave came back from the depths of the 2022 DeFi collapse to become the dominant onchain lending protocol. Today, Aave manages over $60B in TVL, generates $130M in annual revenue, and controls nearly 74% of the lending market. The DAO is phasing out underperforming L2 deployments and “friendly forks,” while doubling down on its stablecoin GHO, buybacks, and strategic growth deals. With its financial strength and sharpened focus, Aave aims to cement its position as the definitive leader in decentralized finance.

articles.title: PROTOCOL RETURNS STILL OUTPACED BY ISSUANCE COSTS
articles.summary: Protocols like Aerodrome return funds to token holders, but the cost of new token issuance still exceeds these returns. This means token holders are effectively losing money for now, which is reasonable if treated as a growth bet, but not to be confused with actual profitability.

articles.title: WHERE THE SEI OPPORTUNITIES ARE
articles.summary: Sei Network's TVL surged from $411M in April to $609M by June, with a sharp increase in active addresses. The ecosystem centers around a "liquidity triangle" of three anchor protocols: DragonSwap offering 30-70% APY on SEI-USDC pools, Yei Finance controlling 44% of Sei's TVL as the dominant money market with 9-12% borrow rates, and Splashing providing liquid staking with $25.5M TVL and 7-9% native yields plus MEV rewards. Institutional momentum is building through the SEC's formal acknowledgment of a Canary Staked SEI ETF filing and Chainlink Data Streams integration, delivering US Department of Commerce GDP data to Sei's infrastructure. Performance metrics show that Sei processes 200x more throughput than Solana and 5x more than Sui, with $5.5B daily stablecoin volume and $1.53B DEX volume in July.

articles.title: FIDELITY'S TOKENIZED MONEY MARKET FUND HITS $200M
articles.summary: Fidelity's tokenized money market fund on Ethereum has quickly grown past $200 million in supply.

articles.title: SYND'S COMMUNITY-FIRST LAUNCH BREAKS RECORDS
articles.summary: The launch of SYND on Aerodrome has attracted unprecedented participation with over $60M in voting power within 4 days, making it the 4th largest pool by votes.

articles.title: BANK OF ENGLAND PROPOSES £10K–£20K STABLECOIN CAP
articles.summary: The Bank of England is considering a cap on individual stablecoin holdings, restricting ownership to £10,000–£20,000 per person to mitigate systemic risk.

articles.title: BITCOIN WHALE CONTINUES SELLING BTC AFTER $4B ETH ROTATION
articles.summary: The whale that swapped over $4B in BTC for ETH in August has resumed selling, moving 1,176 BTC ($136M) to Hyperliquid across two transactions.
